articles.title: Assessment Data
articles.description: This page is currently under construction. The purpose of this page will be to highlight the numbers of assessments performed by EIP staff, and breakdown some information gathered such as risk, substance use, mental health, and other data.
articles.contents: The diagram below is an example of the flow within the Risk/Needs Assessment. This is still under evaluation for the best way to present this information. For example, it could be combined into a full flow diagram of the EIP Database, which would be presented in a different way and in a different section of this website.

articles.title: EIP Service Center
articles.description: This page shows data regarding the CRC screening efforts for EIP.
articles.contents:
The EIP Service Center started on May 3rd, 2023.
In order to fit with their ED General Screening process, the CRCs select the EIP Service Center screening protocol when the patient is at least 15 years or older. The table below shows the total number of screens that have occurred since the start of the EIP Service Center (based on number of entries into the ED General Screen), as well as the total number of patients that were eligible for the EIP Service Center screening protocol based on their age (15 years or older). The percentage of the total screened who are eligible for the EIP Service Center is presented in the table. Finally, the table shows the total number that were marked for the EIP Service Center, and the percentage of the total eligible based on their age.



Screening encounters are excluded from the total eligible for the EIP Service Center if they were unwilling to answer questions during the ED General Screen. The total number of encounters where the patient was unwilling to answer questions at this stage was 0, and thus excluded from the total number eligible for the EIP Service Center.
Total numbers are tracked daily for all ED General Screening done by the CRCs, as well as the daily numbers for those that are marked for the EIP Service Center Screen. The following two graphs show these daily numbers. The first graph and subsequent table shows the daily count of the number of screening encounters done by the CRCs, and the number of screening encounters where the patient was marked as eligible for the EIP Service Center.




The following graph shows the daily percentage of the total eligible for the EIP Service Center based on age that are marked for the EIP Service Center on the ED General Screen.



The CRCs continue with the EIP-specific EPIC pre-screen, looking for various criteria that would indicate that the CRC could approach the patient to gather more information regarding risk and need for EIP action. The graph below shows the number of screens that met the various criteria, and the percentage of the total eligible based on age.



The risk groups that the CRCs are looking for during their Epic pre-screen include: African-American males between the ages of 15-29, Women of Color (WOC), and individuals who identify within the transgender or non-binary community.
The percentages shown in the table below are the percent of the total marked for EIP screening based on age (not all eligible to be screened). The only exceptions are the percentage of those HIV+ not in care (which is a percentage of the total identified as HIV+), and the percentage of those positive for Syphilis in the past year (which is a percentage of the total with a STI in the past year).



This table shows the total number of screening encounters where the patient met at least one criteria for approach by the CRC, and the percent of the total eligible for the EIP Service Center screening based on age. Also shown are the total number of encounters where the patient was approached and consented to discussing additional risk questions, as well as the percentage of the total eligible for approach.

articles.title: EIP Population
articles.description: The purpose of this page is to show the demographic population data for those individuals that EIP has worked with for various clinical purposes. This page shows the entire EIP popluation data, and the population data for individuals seen at specific sites is shown on those pages listed under the Sites tab.
articles.contents:
Contents
Age Distribution
Sex and Gender Identity
Race and Ethnicity
Insurance Coverage
Populations of Interest

The information on this page only includes data since the start of 2023. EIP has encountered a total of 2115 unique individuals at all sites.
The demographic information for this EIP population is shown on this page. For further information regarding the population demographics of individuals at particular sites, please refer to those pages listed under the Site tab in the navigation bar.
Age Distribution

Since age is based on the individual’s date of birth (DOB) and the date of their encounter, this can change the individual’s age over time with various EIP encounters. The data here is shown only for 2023, so age distribution can be presented. When this information is combined with historical EIP data, age distribution will be based on encounter-level data, rather than patient-level data.

The following histogram shows the complete age distribution of all individuals that EIP has encountered, with the density curve overlying the histogram.



This histogram shows the ages of individuals seen by EIP grouped by age categories. The age categories in this histogram were chosen because they are the categories used in both the CTR and EHE reports to ODH. The total numbers of individuals within each category as well as the percent of the total individuals encountered by EIP are shown in the table.






This shows the EIP patient population’s age distribution by sex, excluding those with an unknown or unassigned sex at birth.



This shows the EIP patient population’s age category stratified by sex, excluding those with an unknown or undetermined sex at birth.



The following two histograms also show the overall age distribution of the EIP population. They also allow the ability to filter and select based on self-reported gender (first histogram) and the self-reported race (second histogram). You can filter the information by selecting/deselecting the gender/race from the legend on the right side of the graph. If multiple categories are selected, the histogram shows the cumulative total for each age among the categories selected. If you just want to see the particular age distribution for a single gender/race, you can deselect all other options.






Sex and Gender Identity
EIP collects an individual’s sex assigned at birth as well as their self-defined gender identity. The chart below shows the data regarding the individual’s sex assigned at birth, with the table and percentages also provided below the chart.




The following interactive graph allows for the ability to filter the age category of EIP individuals based on their sex assigned at birth.



For an individual’s gender identity, EIP staff allow the individual to self-report their current gender. This information is shown below in the chart and the subsequent table expressing the percentage of all EIP patients/clients.




The following chart allows for the ability to filter the individual’s age category based on their self-reported gender identity.



Race and Ethnicity
Placeholder for data on race and ethnicity data
Insurance Coverage
Placeholder for data on insurance coverage.
Populations of Interest
Placeholder for data on populations of interest.

articles.title: EIP Data Website
articles.description: This site is currently under construction. The plan for this page is a general introduction to EIP and the data/data sources, the EIP mission statement, and other general info.
articles.contents:
Timeline of EIP
This is an example of the potential timeline that can be included to document the history of EIP, when things were introduced, when research projects were conducted, etc. You can scroll to zoom in and out of the timeline, as well as drag to focus on particular time points or projects. Hovering over the specific text will give you exact dates and/or date ranges of each item.



+
-
